tu.prop: Whole-poem fallback because Chinese and English stanza structures do not align safely.

tuv.seg: 幡幡瓠葉，采之亨之。君子有酒，酌言嘗之。 有兔斯首，炮之燔之。君子有酒，酌言獻之。 有兔斯首，燔之炙之。君子有酒，酌言酢之。 有兔斯首，燔之炮之。君子有酒，酌言醻之。

1 Of the gourd leaves, waving about, Some are taken and boiled; [Then] the superior man, from his spirits, Pours out a cup, and tastes it.
2 There is but a single rabbit, Baked, or roasted. [But] the superior man, from his spirits, Fills the cup and presents it [to his guests].
3 There is but a single rabbit, Roasted, or broiled. [But] from the spirits of the superior man, [His guests] fill the cup, and present it to him.
4 There is but a single rabbit, Roasted or baked. But] from the spirits of the superior man, 'His guests and he] fill the cup and pledge one another.
